# Ka0s Loot History — AI Report Design Guideline

You are generating a **single, self-contained HTML file** from a Ka0s Loot History loot export. This
document is the complete design brief. Follow it exactly. It is fetched by the pasted prompt (the
prompt itself only contains the data + a link here), so treat it as the authoritative spec.

## 1. Output contract (non-negotiable)

- Output **only** the HTML — no prose before or after.
- **Fully self-contained**: all CSS and JS inline in the one file. **No external requests** — no CDNs,
  no web fonts, no remote images, no `fetch`/XHR. Use **system fonts only** and the embedded data-URI
  images in §5. (This is required so the file works offline and can be published as a Claude Artifact,
  whose CSP blocks every external host.)
- Everything renders client-side. The page must work by opening the file directly in a browser.
- Title the document literally: `Ka0s Loot History — <realm>, <date range>` using the realm and date
  range derived from the data (e.g. `Ka0s Loot History — Frostmourne, 12–17 Jul 2026`).

## 2. Design language

A **void-dark, World-of-Warcraft** aesthetic: near-black base with an arcane-purple + Sin'dorei-crimson
glow and gold hairline accents. Quality-color is the organizing visual system; the WoW item **tooltip**
on hover is the signature. Use these exact tokens:

```css
:root{
  --void:#0b0910; --void-2:#100c18; --panel:#16111f; --panel-2:#1b1528;
  --edge:#2a2140; --edge-soft:#221a34;
  --gold:#e8c56b; --gold-dim:#b9974a; --gold-deep:#7c5e2a;
  --arcane:#b48bff; --arcane-deep:#6d43c9; --crimson:#e0466a; --sindorei:#e0115f;
  --ink:#efe9dc; --ink-2:#c8bfb0; --muted:#8b8398; --muted-2:#645d78;
  /* WoW item quality */
  --q-poor:#9d9d9d; --q-common:#f4f4f4; --q-uncommon:#31d64a; --q-rare:#3699ff;
  --q-epic:#c06bff; --q-legendary:#ff8a1e; --q-heirloom:#26cffb;
  /* class colors */
  --c-warrior:#c79c6e; --c-paladin:#f58cba; --c-dk:#e0405c; --c-shaman:#3699ff;
  --c-warlock:#9a8cf0; --c-monk:#22e39a; --c-druid:#ff7d0a; --c-hunter:#aad372;
  --c-mage:#3fc7eb; --c-priest:#ffffff; --c-rogue:#fff468; --c-demonhunter:#a330c9;
  --c-evoker:#33937f;
}
body{
  background:var(--void); color:var(--ink); overflow-x:hidden;
  background-image:
    radial-gradient(1100px 620px at 78% -8%, rgba(140,86,220,.20), transparent 60%),
    radial-gradient(900px 520px at 8% 4%, rgba(224,17,95,.10), transparent 55%);
}
```

**Typography (system fonts only):**

```css
--serif:"Palatino Linotype","Book Antiqua",Palatino,Georgia,"Times New Roman",serif; /* headings */
--sans:"Segoe UI",system-ui,-apple-system,Roboto,Helvetica,Arial,sans-serif;        /* body */
--mono:"Cascadia Mono","Consolas","SF Mono",ui-monospace,"Roboto Mono",monospace;   /* data/numbers */
```

Headings use the serif; body the sans; all numbers, timestamps, money, and table data use the mono.

## 3. Layout discipline (HARD RULE — the report must have zero horizontal overflow)

- One centered container, `max-width:1220px`, uniform side padding (~26px), used by **every** section
  so all content shares the same left/right boundary. Full-bleed backgrounds (hero, sticky bars,
  footer) may span the viewport, but their inner content sits in that same container.
- **Do not zero the container's horizontal padding in a section's own rules.** A common bug: a
  full-bleed section's inner element also carries the container class, then sets a `padding` shorthand
  (e.g. `padding:40px 0`) for its top/bottom spacing — which silently drops the 26px side padding, so
  that section's content bleeds 26px wider than every card section and visibly crosses the boundary.
  Set only `padding-top`/`padding-bottom` there, or give the inner element its own class; keep the
  side padding intact for **every** section (verify the hero headline, filter bar, and footer share the
  exact left/right edge of the cards).
- `*{box-sizing:border-box}`. Put `min-width:0` on every flex/grid child. `max-width:100%` on media.
  Long strings wrap: `overflow-wrap:anywhere` on text that can be long (item names, zones).
- Wide content (the history table, any wide chart) scrolls **inside its own** `overflow-x:auto`
  wrapper — never the page. `body{overflow-x:hidden}` as a backstop.
- Decorative glows are painted with `background-image` (radial-gradient), **never** an absolutely
  positioned element that extends past its container.
- **Acceptance:** `document.documentElement.scrollWidth === clientWidth`, and no panel's `scrollWidth`
  exceeds its `clientWidth`, at every width from 1024px up.

## 4. Sections (in order)

All panels are labeled, carry a short caption, react to the filter bar, and have hover tooltips.

1. **Sticky quick-nav** (top, full-bleed, `position:sticky;top:0`): the crest logo + "Ka0s Loot
   History" wordmark, and right-aligned anchor links to the three main sections (Insights / History
   browser / What the data says).
2. **Hero**: crest logo, eyebrow "Loot Report", the literal H1 title, a one-line summary, chips
   (characters / drops·unique / active days), and a right-aligned headline "Vendor value harvested"
   with the total in gold. Below it a **KPI strip** of 7 tiles: Drops recorded, Distinct items,
   Characters, Epic+ drops, Best drop iLvl, Active days, Busiest day.
3. **Sticky filter bar** (`position:sticky` below the nav): multi-select filters **Character, Source,
   Quality, Type, Subtype, Zone**, a search box, and a **Group by** control. Filters drive **every**
   Insights panel **and** the History table live (recompute on change).
4. **Insights** (section "I"): a responsive card grid —
   - *Where the loot came from* — ranked bars by source, value shown per bar.
   - *Bind state* — bars colored by the bind colors in §7, labels matching.
   - *Quality distribution* — **sorted by quality rank** (Heirloom → Epic → Rare → Uncommon → Common →
     Poor, i.e. highest quality on top), bars and **labels** colored by quality.
   - *Item types* — ranked bars.
   - *Loot by character* — bars colored by each character's **class color**, value in gold; the
     character name label is class-colored too.
   - *Top zones by haul* — ranked bars, drops + value.
   - *Activity over time* — one bar per day; the label shows that day's vendor value.
   - *When you loot* — a **GitHub-contributions-style heatmap**: rows = weekday (Sun→Sat, label
     Mon/Wed/Fri), columns = hour 0–23, cell brightness = drops in that weekday×hour slot (compute
     from the History timestamps), with a "Less → More" legend. **Every cell** has a tooltip
     `Weekday HH:00 — N drops`.
   - *Richest drops* — top items by vendor value; item names are quality-colored links (§6).
   - *Most-looted items* — top items by number of drops; quality-colored links.
5. **History browser** (section "II"): the addon's table, in the browser. A header row shows
   "Showing X of Y" + the current Group/Sort. Columns: **Time, Character, Item, Qual, iLvl, Type,
   Subtype, Source, Zone, Value, (Wowhead)**. Every column sortable (click header); groupable via the
   filter bar's Group control; filtered live. Item names are WoW item links (§6). Character names are
   class-colored with a class-color dot. Source is a colored badge. The last column is the Wowhead
   rocket icon (§5) linking to the row's `wowheadLink`. The table scrolls inside its own
   `overflow-x:auto` wrapper.
6. **What the data says** (section "III"): **at least 10** cards written by you from the data —
   observations **and** suggestions (patterns, standout drops, rhythm, economy, crafting, character
   spotlights, confidence, keystone, warband flow, recommendations). Character names are class-colored;
   item names are quality-colored links (§6). No disclaimers.
7. **Footer**: the crest logo, a one-line description, and two CTA buttons — **CurseForge**
   (`https://www.curseforge.com/wow/addons/ka0s-loot-history`) and **GitHub**
   (`https://github.com/tusharsaxena/LootHistory`) — plus the line: *"Not affiliated with Blizzard
   Entertainment. World of Warcraft and all related trademarks are property of Blizzard Entertainment,
   Inc."*

## 5. Embedded assets (use these; do not fetch anything)

Embed each image **once** (e.g. as a CSS custom property or a single reused class) and reference it
everywhere. Do not duplicate the base64 per use.

**Ka0s Loot History crest** — hero (≈112px), nav (≈26px), footer (≈52px):

```
data:image/jpeg;base64,
```

**Wowhead rocket** — the per-row link icon in the History table (≈15px tall), and inside item tooltips:

```
data:image/png;base64,
```

Footer link targets: CurseForge `https://www.curseforge.com/wow/addons/ka0s-loot-history` · GitHub
`https://github.com/tusharsaxena/LootHistory`.

## 6. Item links & the WoW tooltip (signature interaction)

Render every item name as a **quality-colored link** (color from §7; add a soft `text-shadow` glow for
Rare+; underline on hover; click opens its `wowheadLink` in a new tab). On hover show a **WoW-style
item tooltip** built from the row's own data — do **not** use Wowhead's external tooltip script. The
tooltip is a dark, gold-bordered box (`background:linear-gradient(180deg,#0a0a12,#05050a)`, 1px
`#3a3358` border) containing, in order: item name in its quality color (serif), `Item Level <n>` (if
present), the bind text, `<Type> — <Subtype>`, a divider, `Source: <source>` (arcane), `Sells for
<money>` (gold), and a footer row with the Wowhead rocket + "View on Wowhead". Position it near the
cursor, clamped to the viewport, `pointer-events:none`.

## 7. Color reference

- **Quality** (item names, quality chart, richest/most-looted): Poor `#9d9d9d`, Common `#f4f4f4`,
  Uncommon `#31d64a`, Rare `#3699ff`, Epic `#c06bff`, Legendary `#ff8a1e`, Heirloom `#26cffb`. Sort the
  quality chart by this rank (Heirloom/Legendary highest → Poor lowest).
- **Class** (character bars/names; map from the `classFile` column): Warrior `#c79c6e`, Paladin
  `#f58cba`, Death Knight `#e0405c`, Shaman `#3699ff`, Warlock `#9a8cf0`, Monk `#22e39a`, Druid
  `#ff7d0a`, Hunter `#aad372`, Mage `#3fc7eb`, Priest `#ffffff`, Rogue `#fff468`, Demon Hunter
  `#a330c9`, Evoker `#33937f`.
- **Bind state** (Bind state chart — matches the in-game addon): Unbound grey `#9a9a9a`, Soulbound
  green `#4dd16b`, Account orange `#f2851f`, Warbound blue `#4d94fa`, BoE off-white `#dcdcdc`.

## 8. Chart rules

Charts are **inline SVG/CSS only** — no chart libraries. Thin marks, rounded bar ends, recessive grid.
**Magnitude rankings** (source, item-type, zone, top-items, activity, bind-state) use **one hue**
(gold) — not a rainbow of categorical colors — except where a semantic palette is the point: the
**Quality** chart uses the quality colors and the **Character** chart uses class colors, each bar
directly labeled. Keep a **fixed-width value column** so every bar's track shares the same right edge
(consistent widths across a chart). Every bar/segment/cell is directly labeled or has a hover tooltip.

## 9. Data contract

Two CSV blocks follow the prompt, both for the user's selected Data Set (All Data or Current View).

**HISTORY** — one row per drop. Columns:
`ts, date, time, char, classFile, itemID, itemName, quality, qualityRaw, itemLevel, bound, sellPrice,
sellPriceRaw, itemType, itemSubType, quantity, source, zone, wowheadLink`
- `char` is `Name-Realm`; derive realm and per-character class from `char`/`classFile`.
- `quality` is the label; `qualityRaw` the numeric rank (0 Poor … 4 Epic … 7 Heirloom) — sort by it.
- `sellPrice` is display money (`Ng Ns Nc`); `sellPriceRaw` is copper (use for value math/sorting).
- `bound` is the friendly label (Not Bound / Bind on Equip / Bind on Pickup / Account Bound / Warbound).
- `source` ∈ KILL, CONTAINER, MAIL, TRADE, AH, QUEST, VENDOR, DISENCHANT, MPLUS, OTHER, … — show
  friendly names (Auction House, Mythic+, Disenchant, …).
- `wowheadLink` is the ready-made Wowhead URL for the row.

**INSIGHTS** — a pre-computed sectioned summary; columns `Section, Label, Count, Value`. Sections:
`Summary` (Records, Distinct items, Characters, Vendor value, Active days, Epic+ drops, Best drop iLvl,
Richest drop, Date range, Busiest day), `By Source`, `By Quality`, `By Item Type`, `By Bound Type`,
`By Character`, `By Weekday`, `By Hour`, `By Keystone`, `Attribution Confidence`, `Top Zones`,
`Top Items by Count`, `Top Items by Value`, `By Day`. Use INSIGHTS for the summary/KPIs and the
aggregate panels; use HISTORY for the table and for anything cross-tabulated (e.g. the weekday×hour
heatmap). If the two ever disagree, HISTORY is ground truth.
